Name: Weedle
Flavor-Text: Its poison stinger is very powerful. Its bright-colored body is intended to warn off its enemies.

Name: Kakuna
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 40 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: While awaiting evolution, it hides from predators under leaves and in nooks of branches.

Name: Beedrill
Effects: If your opponent’s Active Pokémon has any Special Energy attached, it is Knocked Out.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: May appear in a swarm. Flies at violent speeds, all the while stabbing with the toxic stinger on its rear.

Name: Ledyba
Effects: Draw a card.
Flavor-Text: These very cowardly Pokémon join together and use Reflect to protect their nest.

Name: Ledian
Effects: Draw 2 cards.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It flies through the night sky, sprinkling sparkly dust. According to some, if that dust sticks to you, good things will happen to you.

Name: Heracross
Effects: Flip 2 coins. If both of them are heads, this attack does 160 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It roams through forests searching for sweet nectar. Although it boasts fantastic physical strength, it’s not that good at flying.

Name: Celebi V
Effects: Attach any number of [G] Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.
Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Celebi VMAX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may heal 20 damage from each of your [G] Pokémon.
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Pokémon, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Snover
Flavor-Text: The berries that grow around its belly are like ice pops. Galarian Darumaka absolutely love these berries.

Name: Abomasnow
Ability-Effect: Your Single Strike Pokémon in play, except any Abomasnow, get +50 HP. You can’t apply more than 1 Toughness Boost Ability at a time.
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon is known to bring blizzards. A shake of its massive body is enough to cause whiteout conditions.

Name: Deerling
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon’s attacks do 20 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: The turning of the seasons changes the color and scent of this Pokémon’s fur. People use it to mark the seasons.

Name: Sawsbuck
Effects: If you have a Stadium in play, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: They migrate according to the seasons. People can tell the season by looking at Sawsbuck’s horns.

Name: Bounsweet
Flavor-Text: When under attack, it secretes a sweet and delicious sweat. The scent only calls more enemies to it.

Name: Steenee
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Any Corvisquire that pecks at this Pokémon will be greeted with a smack from its sepals followed by a sharp kick.

Name: Tsareena
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each [C] in your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost.
Flavor-Text: A kick from the hardened tips of this Pokémon’s legs leaves a wound in the opponent’s body and soul that will never heal.

Name: Grookey
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: It attacks with rapid beats of its stick. As it strikes with amazing speed, it gets more and more pumped.

Name: Thwackey
Effects: Discard a random card from your opponent’s hand.
Flavor-Text: When it’s drumming out rapid beats in battle, it gets so caught up in the rhythm that it won’t even notice that it’s already knocked out its opponent.

Name: Rillaboom
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Discard any amount of Energy from your Pokémon. This attack does 30 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.
Flavor-Text: The one with the best drumming techniques becomes the boss of the troop. It has a gentle disposition and values harmony among its group.

Name: Zarude
Effects: Search your deck for a [G] Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. If you go second and it’s your first turn, search for up to 3 [G] Pokémon instead of 1. Then, shuffle your deck.
This attack does 20 more damage for each [G] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Once the vines on Zarude’s body tear off, they become nutrients in the soil. This helps the plants of the forest grow.

Name: Blaziken V
Effects: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Blaziken VMAX
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon can’t retreat.
Choose up to 2 of your Benched Rapid Strike Pokémon and attach an Energy card from your discard pile to each of them.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Castform Sunny Form
Ability-Effect: If you have 8 or more Stadium cards in your discard pile, ignore all Energy in this Pokémon’s attack costs.
Effects: Discard a Stadium in play. If you can’t, this attack does nothing.
Flavor-Text: Castform changes to this form when it basks in bright sunlight. When you touch its glowing skin, it feels all dried out!

Name: Larvesta
Effects: Search your deck for a [R] Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It protects itself with flame. Long years ago, people believed Larvesta had a nest on the sun.

Name: Volcarona
Effects: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: According to legends, it was hatched from a flaming cocoon to save people and Pokémon that were suffering from the cold.

Name: Volcanion V
Effects: If this Pokémon has 10 or more damage counters on it, this attack does 150 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Scorbunny
Flavor-Text: It has special pads on the backs of its feet, and one on its nose. Once it’s raring to fight, these pads radiate tremendous heat.

Name: Raboot
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: It kicks berries right off the branches of trees and then juggles them with its feet, practicing its footwork.

Name: Cinderace
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon’s attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon for each Prize card your opponent has taken (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: It’s skilled at both offense and defense, and it gets pumped up when cheered on. But if it starts showboating, it could put itself in a tough spot.

Name: Lapras
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 Melony cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: Crossing icy seas is no issue for this cold-resistant Pokémon. Its smooth skin is a little cool to the touch.

Name: Sneasel
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It has a cunning yet savage disposition. It waits for parents to leave their nests, and then it sneaks in to steal their eggs.

Name: Weavile
Effects: During your next turn, if the Defending Pokémon is damaged by an attack from a Rapid Strike Pokémon, it will be Knocked Out.
Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: With its claws, it leaves behind signs for its friends to find. The number of distinct signs is said to be over 500.

Name: Delibird
Effects: Put this Pokémon and all attached cards into your deck. If you do, search your deck for a card and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It has a generous habit of sharing its food with people and Pokémon, so it’s always scrounging around for more food.

Name: Castform Rainy Form
Ability-Effect: If you have 8 or more Stadium cards in your discard pile, ignore all Energy in this Pokémon’s attack costs.
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: This is Castform’s form when pelted by rain. In an experiment where it was placed in a shower, this Pokémon didn’t change to this form.

Name: Castform Snowy Form
Ability-Effect: If you have 8 or more Stadium cards in your discard pile, ignore all Energy in this Pokémon’s attack costs.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t use Frosty Typhoon.
Flavor-Text: This is Castform’s form when caught in a hailstorm. Its whole body is chilled, and its skin is partially frozen!

Name: Snorunt
Flavor-Text: It can only survive in cold areas. It bounces happily around, even in environments as cold as −150 degrees Fahrenheit.

Name: Froslass
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand to evolve 1 of your Pokémon during your turn, you may attach a [W] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon.
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: It spits out cold air of nearly −60 degrees Fahrenheit to freeze its quarry. It brings frozen prey back to its lair and neatly lines them up.

Name: Spheal
Flavor-Text: Its body is covered in fluffy fur. The fur keeps it from feeling cold while it is rolling on ice.

Name: Sealeo
Flavor-Text: Be it Spheal or a Poké Ball, it will spin any round object on its nose with the greatest of ease.

Name: Walrein
Effects: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon. Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Flavor-Text: It shatters drift ice with its strong tusks. Its thick layer of blubber repels enemy attacks.

Name: Tapu Fini
Effects: You may switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Put an Energy attached to this Pokémon into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Although it’s called a guardian deity, terrible calamities sometimes befall those who recklessly approach Tapu Fini.

Name: Sobble
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Rapid Strike Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: When it gets wet, its skin changes color, and this Pokémon becomes invisible as if it were camouflaged.

Name: Drizzile
Effects: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Highly intelligent but also very lazy, it keeps enemies out of its territory by laying traps everywhere.

Name: Inteleon
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may put 2 damage counters on 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Its nictitating membranes let it pick out foes’ weak points so it can precisely blast them with water that shoots from its fingertips at Mach 3.

Name: Rapid Strike Urshifu
Effects: This attack does 30 damage for each of your Rapid Strike Pokémon in play.
Flavor-Text: This form of Urshifu is a strong believer in defeating foes by raining many blows down on them. Its strikes are nonstop, flowing like a river.

Name: Ice Rider Calyrex V
Effects: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Ice Rider Calyrex VMAX
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon.
You may discard up to 2 Energy from this Pokémon. If you do, this attack does 120 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Mareep
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon’s attacks do 20 less damage (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Rubbing its fleece generates electricity. You’ll want to pet it because it’s cute, but if you use your bare hand, you’ll get a painful shock.

Name: Flaaffy
Flavor-Text: It stores electricity in its fluffy fleece. If it stores up too much, it will start to go bald in those patches.

Name: Ampharos
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Your opponent reveals their hand. If you find any Energy cards there, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Its tail shines bright and strong. It has been prized since long ago as a beacon for sailors.

Name: Blitzle
Effects: This attack does 10 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Its mane shines when it discharges electricity. They use the frequency and rhythm of these flashes to communicate.

Name: Zebstrika
Effects: If 1 of your other Rapid Strike Pokémon used an attack during your last turn, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 90 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: They have lightning-like movements. When Zebstrika run at full speed, the sound of thunder reverberates.

Name: Thundurus
Effects: You may attach a [L] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: As it flies around, it shoots lightning all over the place and causes forest fires. It is therefore disliked.

Name: Zeraora V
Effects: If 1 of your other Rapid Strike Pokémon used an attack during your last turn, this attack also does 160 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Galarian Slowpoke
Flavor-Text: Because Galarian Slowpoke eat the seeds of a plant that grows only in Galar, their tails have developed a spicy flavor.

Name: Gastly
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Asleep.
Flavor-Text: With its gas-like body, it can sneak into any place it desires. However, it can be blown away by wind.

Name: Haunter
Flavor-Text: If you get the feeling of being watched in darkness when nobody is around, Haunter is there.

Name: Gengar
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon, search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Effects: This attack does 40 more damage for each damage counter on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It is said to emerge from darkness to steal the lives of those who become lost in mountains.

Name: Galarian Articuno V
Ability-Effect: You must discard 2 cards from your hand in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw a card.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Ralts
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: If its horns capture the warm feelings of people or Pokémon, its body warms up slightly.

Name: Kirlia
Effects: Search your deck for up to 3 Kirlia and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: It has a psychic power that enables it to distort the space around it and see into the future.

Name: Gardevoir
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may look at the top 2 cards of your deck and attach any number of basic Energy cards you find there to your Pokémon in any way you like. Put the other cards into your hand.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each [P] Energy attached to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: To protect its Trainer, it will expend all its psychic power to create a small black hole.

Name: Shuppet
Flavor-Text: There’s a proverb that says, “Shun the house where Shuppet gather in the growing dusk.”

Name: Banette
Effects: Put up to 7 damage counters on this Pokémon. This attack does 20 damage for each damage counter you placed in this way.
Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused.
Flavor-Text: Resentment at being cast off made it spring into being. Some say that treating it well will satisfy it, and it will once more become a stuffed toy.

Name: Cresselia
Effects: Search your deck for a [P] Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Pokémon. If you go second and it’s your first turn, instead search for up to 3 [P] Energy cards and attach them to 1 of your Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
If you have at least 5 Energy in play, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Those who sleep holding Cresselia’s feather are assured of joyful dreams. It is said to represent the crescent moon.

Name: Golett
Flavor-Text: This Pokémon was created from clay. It received orders from its master many thousands of years ago, and it still follows those orders to this day.

Name: Golurk
Effects: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 90 more damage.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: There’s a theory that inside Golurk is a perpetual motion machine that produces limitless energy, but this belief hasn’t been proven.

Name: Swirlix
Effects: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: The sweet smell of cotton candy perfumes Swirlix’s fluffy fur. This Pokémon spits out sticky string to tangle up its enemies.

Name: Slurpuff
Effects: Flip 3 coins. Put a number of cards up to the number of heads from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: Slurpuff’s fur contains a lot of air, making it soft to the touch and lighter than it looks.

Name: Inkay
Flavor-Text: By exposing foes to the blinking of its luminescent spots, Inkay demoralizes them, and then it seizes the chance to flee.

Name: Malamar
Effects: Reveal any number of Rapid Strike cards from your hand. This attack does 40 damage for each card you revealed in this way. Then, shuffle those cards into your deck.
Flavor-Text: It’s said that Malamar’s hypnotic powers played a role in certain history-changing events.

Name: Hatenna
Flavor-Text: If this Pokémon senses a strong emotion, it will run away as fast as it can. It prefers areas without people.

Name: Hattrem
Effects: Heal 30 damage from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Using the braids on its head, it pummels foes to get them to quiet down. One blow from those braids would knock out a professional boxer.

Name: Hatterene
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may switch your Active Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon. If you do, your opponent switches their Active Pokémon with 1 of their Benched Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: If you’re too loud around it, you risk being torn apart by the claws on its tentacle. This Pokémon is also known as the Forest Witch.

Name: Shadow Rider Calyrex V
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, they can’t play any Special Energy or Stadium cards from their hand.
Choose 2 of your opponent’s Pokémon and put 5 damage counters on each of them.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Shadow Rider Calyrex VMAX
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a [P] Energy card from your hand to 1 of your Benched [P] Pokémon. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, draw 2 cards.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each [P] Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Diglett
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: It burrows through the ground at a shallow depth. It leaves raised earth in its wake, making it easy to spot.

Name: Dugtrio
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 60 damage for each heads. If all of them are heads, during your opponent’s next turn, prevent all damage from and effects of attacks done to this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: These Diglett triplets dig over 60 miles below sea level. No one knows what it’s like underground.

Name: Galarian Farfetch’d
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: The stalks of leeks are thicker and longer in the Galar region. Farfetch’d that adapted to these stalks took on a unique form.

Name: Galarian Sirfetch’d
Effects: If this Pokémon has a Pokémon Tool attached, this attack does 90 more damage, and this attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
Flavor-Text: After deflecting attacks with its hard leaf shield, it strikes back with its sharp leek stalk. The leek stalk is both weapon and food.

Name: Galarian Zapdos V
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon’s attacks cost [C] less for each of your opponent’s Pokémon V in play.
Effects: Before doing damage, discard a Special Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Gallade
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Resistance.
This attack does 60 damage for each of your opponent’s Pokémon V in play.
Flavor-Text: Sharply attuned to others’ wishes for help, this Pokémon seeks out those in need and aids them in battle.

Name: Galarian Yamask
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 30 more damage.
Flavor-Text: It’s said that this Pokémon was formed when an ancient clay tablet was drawn to a vengeful spirit.

Name: Galarian Runerigus
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon VMAX (even if this Pokémon is Knocked Out), put damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon equal to the damage done to this Pokémon.
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Never touch its shadowlike body, or you’ll be shown the horrific memories behind the picture carved into it.

Name: Crabrawler
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Crabrawler has been known to mistake Exeggutor for a coconut tree and climb it. The enraged Exeggutor shakes it off and stomps it.

Name: Crabominable
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 90 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Before it stops to think, it just starts pummeling. There are records of its turning back avalanches with a flurry of punches.

Name: Rockruff
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: When it rubs the rocks on its neck against you, that’s proof of its love for you. However, the rocks are sharp, so the gesture is quite painful!

Name: Lycanroc
Effects: This attack does 10 more damage for each Single Strike Pokémon in your discard pile.
Flavor-Text: They live alone without forming packs. They will only listen to orders from Trainers who can draw out their true power.

Name: Passimian
Ability-Effect: Your Rapid Strike Pokémon’s attacks do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Benched Pokémon V and Benched Pokémon-GX (before applying Weakness and Resistance). You can’t apply more than 1 Throwing Coach Ability at a time.
Effects: This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Passimian live in groups of about 20, with each member performing an assigned role. Through cooperation, the group survives.

Name: Sandaconda V
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Sandaconda VMAX
Effects: This attack also does 20 damage to each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Move any amount of Energy from your Pokémon to your other Pokémon in any way you like.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Clobbopus
Flavor-Text: Its tentacles tear off easily, but it isn’t alarmed when that happens—it knows they’ll grow back. It’s about as smart as a three-year-old.

Name: Grapploct
Ability-Effect: As long as this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, your opponent’s Active Pokémon’s Retreat Cost is [C][C] more.
Effects: If you have the same number of cards in your hand as your opponent, this attack does 80 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Searching for an opponent to test its skills against, it emerges onto land. Once the battle is over, it returns to the sea.

Name: Kubfu
Effects: Search your deck for a basic Energy card and attach it to this Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.
Flavor-Text: If Kubfu pulls the long white hair on its head, its fighting spirit heightens and power wells up from the depths of its belly.

Name: Koffing
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It adores polluted air. Some claim that Koffing used to be more plentiful in the Galar region than they are now.

Name: Weezing
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Confused. Attach a [D] Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
This attack does 20 more damage for each [D] Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Very rarely, a sudden mutation can result in two small Koffing twins becoming conjoined as a Weezing.

Name: Galarian Weezing
Ability-Effect: Each basic [D] Energy attached to your Pokémon that have “Weezing” in their name provides [D][D] Energy. You can’t apply more than 1 Energy Factory Ability at a time.
Flavor-Text: Long ago, during a time when droves of factories fouled the air with pollution, Weezing changed into this form for some reason.

Name: Galarian Moltres V
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may attach a [D] Energy card from your discard pile to this Pokémon. You can’t use more than 1 Direflame Wings Ability each turn.
Effects: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Galarian Slowking
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, you may choose 1 of your Pokémon and flip a coin. If heads, heal 90 damage from that Pokémon. If tails, put 3 damage counters on that Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: While chanting strange spells, this Pokémon combines its internal toxins with what it’s eaten, creating strange potions.

Name: Galarian Slowking V
Effects: Discard a card from your hand. If you do, draw 3 cards.
At the end of your opponent’s next turn, the Defending Pokémon will be Knocked Out.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Galarian Slowking VMAX
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned. During Pokémon Checkup, put 12 damage counters on that Pokémon instead of 1.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Qwilfish
Ability-Effect: If this Pokémon is in the Active Spot and is Knocked Out by damage from an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon, put 6 damage counters on the Attacking Pokémon.
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: The small spikes covering its body developed from scales. They inject a toxin that causes fainting.

Name: Seviper
Effects: If you played a Single Strike Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack does 90 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Constant polishing makes the edge of the blade on its tail extremely sharp. It’s Zangoose’s archrival.

Name: Spiritomb
Effects: For each Pokémon in your opponent’s discard pile, put 1 damage counter on your opponent’s Pokémon in any way you like. If you placed any damage counters in this way, your opponent shuffles all Pokémon from their discard pile into their deck.
Flavor-Text: It was bound to a fissure in an Odd Keystone as punishment for misdeeds 500 years ago.

Name: Liepard V
Ability-Effect: When you play this Pokémon from your hand onto your Bench during your turn, you may discard a Pokémon Tool from a Pokémon (yours or your opponent’s).
Effects: You may put this Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Venipede
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: Its bite injects a potent poison, enough to paralyze large bird Pokémon that try to prey on it.

Name: Whirlipede
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flavor-Text: It is usually motionless, but when attacked, it rotates at high speed and then crashes into its opponent.

Name: Scolipede
Effects: Your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Poisoned.
If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is Poisoned, this attack does 120 more damage.
Flavor-Text: With quick movements, it chases down its foes, attacking relentlessly with its horns until it prevails.

Name: Single Strike Urshifu
Effects: If your opponent has a Stadium in play, discard it.
If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 100 more damage.
Flavor-Text: Inhabiting the mountains of a distant region, this Pokémon races across sheer cliffs, training its legs and refining its moves.

Name: Aron
Flavor-Text: When it evolves, it sheds the steel carapace that covered its whole body and develops a new one.

Name: Lairon
Flavor-Text: It habitually shows off its strength with the size of sparks it creates by ramming its steel body into boulders.

Name: Aggron
Effects: During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 30 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
This attack also does 30 damage to each of your Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: While seeking iron for food, it digs tunnels by breaking through bedrock with its steel horns.

Name: Metagross V
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 20 more damage for each heads.
If this Pokémon and your opponent’s Active Pokémon have the same amount of Energy attached, this attack does 90 more damage.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Metagross VMAX
Effects: Search your deck for up to 2 cards and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
During your next turn, this Pokémon’s Max Rush attack does 150 more damage.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Cobalion
Effects: During your next turn, this Pokémon can’t attack.
Flavor-Text: It has a body and heart of steel. It worked with its allies to punish people when they hurt Pokémon.

Name: Tauros
Effects: This attack does 20 more damage for each damage counter on this Pokémon. This Pokémon is now Confused.
This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.
Flavor-Text: They live in groups. The one with the longest, thickest, and most-scarred horns is the boss of the herd.

Name: Porygon
Flavor-Text: It was built 20 years ago by scientists who dreamed of exploring space. Their dreams have yet to come true.

Name: Porygon2
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 30 damage for each heads.
Flavor-Text: Even though it doesn’t die in the vacuum of space, it can’t move around very well in zero gravity.

Name: Porygon-Z
Ability-Effect: Whenever you attach an Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon during your turn, you may make your opponent’s Active Pokémon Confused.
Effects: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: A faulty update was added to its programming. Its behavior is noticeably strange, so the experiment may have been a failure.

Name: Blissey V
Ability-Effect: Whenever you attach an Energy card from your hand to this Pokémon, it recovers from all Special Conditions.
Effects: This attack does 30 more damage for each Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you did any damage with this attack, you may attach up to 3 Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Zangoose
Effects: If you played a Rapid Strike Supporter card from your hand during this turn, this attack also does 50 damage to 2 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)
Flavor-Text: Its fur would all stand on end if it smelled a Seviper nearby. Its sharp claws tear up its foes.

Name: Castform
Ability-Effect: If you have 8 or more Stadium cards in your discard pile, ignore all Energy in this Pokémon’s attack costs.
Effects: Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
Flavor-Text: Its form changes depending on the weather. The rougher conditions get, the rougher Castform’s disposition!

Name: Kecleon
Ability-Effect: This Pokémon is the same type as any basic Energy attached to it. (If it has 2 or more different types of basic Energy attached, this Pokémon is each of those types.)
Flavor-Text: Its color changes for concealment and also when its mood or health changes. The darker the color, the healthier it is.

Name: Shaymin
Effects: You may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
Put this Pokémon and all attached cards into your hand.
Flavor-Text: The blooming of Gracidea flowers confers the power of flight upon it. Feelings of gratitude are the message it delivers.

Name: Tornadus V
Effects: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 20 more damage.
Discard an Energy from this Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Name: Tornadus VMAX
Effects: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 120 more damage. Then, discard that Stadium.
VMAX-Rule: When your Pokémon VMAX is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

Name: Furfrou
Effects: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
During your opponent’s next turn, this Pokémon takes 20 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Flavor-Text: Left alone, its fur will grow longer and longer, but it will only allow someone it trusts to cut it.

Name: Skwovet
Effects: Put a Pokémon Tool card from your discard pile into your hand.
Flavor-Text: It eats berries nonstop—a habit that has made it more resilient than it looks. It’ll show up on farms, searching for yet more berries.

Name: Greedent
Ability-Effect: Energy attached to your Pokémon can’t be put into your hand, deck, or discard pile by an effect of your opponent’s Item or Supporter cards.
Flavor-Text: Common throughout the Galar region, this Pokémon has strong teeth and can chew through the toughest of berry shells.

Name: Agatha
Description: Move up to 3 damage counters from your Active Pokémon to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Avery
Description: Draw 3 cards. If you drew any cards in this way, your opponent discards Pokémon from their Bench until they have 3.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Brawly
Description: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Rapid Strike Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Caitlin
Description: Put any number of cards from your hand on the bottom of your deck in any order. Then, draw that many cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Crushing Gloves
Description: The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active [M] Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Doctor
Description: Draw 2 cards. If your opponent’s Active Pokémon is a Pokémon VMAX, draw 2 more cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Dyna Tree Hill
Description: Pokémon (both yours and your opponent’s) can’t be healed.
Stadium-Rule: This Stadium stays in play when you play it. Discard it if another Stadium comes into play. If a Stadium with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Echoing Horn
Description: Put a Basic Pokémon from your opponent’s discard pile onto their Bench.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Expedition Uniform
Description: Look at the bottom 3 cards of your deck and put them on top of your deck in any order.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Fire-Resistant Gloves
Description: The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active [R] Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Flannery
Description: Discard a Special Energy from 1 of your opponent’s Pokémon, and discard a Stadium in play.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Fog Crystal
Description: Search your deck for a [P] Energy card or a Basic [P] Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Galarian Chestplate
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to has “Galarian” in its name, it takes 30 less damage from attacks from your opponent’s Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Honey
Description: Draw a card for each of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon V.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Justified Gloves
Description: The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active [D] Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Karen’s Conviction
Description: During this turn, your Single Strike Pokémon’s attacks do 20 more damage to your opponent’s Active Pokémon for each Prize card your opponent has taken (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Klara
Description:
Choose 1 or both:
• Put up to 2 Pokémon from your discard pile into your hand.
• Put up to 2 basic Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Melony
Description: Attach a [W] Energy card from your discard pile to 1 of your Pokémon V. If you do, draw 3 cards.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Old Cemetery
Description: Whenever any player attaches an Energy card from their hand to 1 of their non-[P] Pokémon, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.
Stadium-Rule: This Stadium stays in play when you play it. Discard it if another Stadium comes into play. If a Stadium with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Path to the Peak
Description: Pokémon with a Rule Box in play (both yours and your opponent’s) have no Abilities. (Pokémon V, Pokémon-GX, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
Stadium-Rule: This Stadium stays in play when you play it. Discard it if another Stadium comes into play. If a Stadium with the same name is in play, you can’t play this card.

Name: Peonia
Description: Put up to 3 Prize cards into your hand. Then, for each Prize card you put into your hand in this way, put a card from your hand face down as a Prize card.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Peony
Description: Discard your hand and search your deck for up to 2 Trainer cards, reveal them, and put them into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Rapid Strike Scroll of the Skies
Effects: This attack does 50 more damage for each Energy attached to your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Description: The Rapid Strike Pokémon this card is attached to can use the attack on this card. (You still need the necessary Energy to use this attack.)
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Rugged Helmet
Description: If the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if it is Knocked Out), put an Energy attached to the Attacking Pokémon into your opponent’s hand.
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Siebold
Description: Choose up to 2 of your Rapid Strike Pokémon and heal 60 damage from each of them.
Supporter-Rule: You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

Name: Single Strike Scroll of Piercing
Effects: This attack’s damage isn’t affected by Weakness or Resistance, or by any effects on your opponent’s Active Pokémon.
Description: The Single Strike Pokémon this card is attached to can use the attack on this card. (You still need the necessary Energy to use this attack.)
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Weeding Gloves
Description: The attacks of the Pokémon this card is attached to do 30 more damage to your opponent’s Active [G] Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
Tool-Rule: Attach a Pokémon Tool to 1 of your Pokémon that doesn’t already have a Pokémon Tool attached.

Name: Welcoming Lantern
Description: Put a Single Strike Supporter card from your discard pile into your hand.
Item-Rule: You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

Name: Impact Energy
Description: This card can only be attached to a Single Strike Pokémon. If this card is attached to anything other than a Single Strike Pokémon, discard this card.As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time. The Pokémon this card is attached to can’t be Poisoned, and if it is already Poisoned, it recovers from that Special Condition.

Name: Lucky Energy
Description: As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides [C] Energy.If the Pokémon this card is attached to is in the Active Spot and is damaged by an attack from your opponent’s Pokémon (even if it is Knocked Out), draw a card.

Name: Spiral Energy
Description: This card can only be attached to a Rapid Strike Pokémon. If this card is attached to anything other than a Rapid Strike Pokémon, discard this card.As long as this card is attached to a Pokémon, it provides every type of Energy but provides only 1 Energy at a time. The Pokémon this card is attached to can’t be Paralyzed, and if it is already Paralyzed, it recovers from that Special Condition.

Name: Celebi V
Effects: Attach any number of [G] Energy cards from your hand to your Pokémon in any way you like.
Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.
V-Rule: When your Pokémon V is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

Effects: If 1 of your other Rapid Strike Pokémon used an attack during your last turn, this attack also does 160 damage to 1 of your opponent’s Benched Pokémon. (Don’t apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Flavor-Text: It stores an overflowing amount of electric energy inside its body. Even a small shock makes it explode.

Name: Bronzong
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn, you may move a [M] Energy from 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon.
Flavor-Text: Many scientists suspect that this Pokémon originated outside the Galar region, based on the patterns on its body.

Name: Snorlax
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is in the Active Spot, you may draw cards until you have 7 cards in your hand. If you use this Ability, your turn ends.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent’s Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
